name: Sebum Balance Watery Toner
reviews: Lightweight and absorbs quickly without a greasy finish.
Helped my oily T-zone feel less shiny by afternoon.
Gentle toner; no fragrance smell and no stinging for me.
evidence_notes: Ingredient list has niacinamide, green tea, panthenol, and glycerin.

name: Pore Clarifying BHA Liquid
reviews: Good for blackheads but can be drying if I use it daily.
My oily skin liked it twice a week.
Some stinging around my nose at first.
evidence_notes: Contains salicylic acid, so frequency and sensitivity matter.

name: Ceramide Barrier Cream
reviews: Very soothing when my skin barrier felt damaged.
Rich but not too greasy at night.
Helped with dry patches and redness.
evidence_notes: Barrier-focused formula with ceramide, panthenol, glycerin, and centella.

name: Green Tea Oil-Control Gel Cream
reviews: Lightweight gel that feels hydrating but not greasy.
My makeup lasted better because it dried down semi-matte.
Not enough for very dry cheeks in winter.
evidence_notes: Uses niacinamide, green tea, zinc PCA, and hyaluronic acid for oily but dehydrated skin.

name: Fragrant Glow Serum
reviews: Nice glow but the fragrance is strong.
Felt sticky under sunscreen.
I stopped using it after some irritation.
evidence_notes: Contains niacinamide, but fragrance components are a sensitivity concern.

name: Gentle Retinol Night Serum
reviews: Improved texture but I had to start slowly.
Can be drying if I skip moisturizer.
I use sunscreen every morning with this.
evidence_notes: Retinol requires caution for irritation and pregnancy/nursing.

name: Fragrance-Free Daily Mineral Sunscreen
reviews: Gentle and calming but leaves a slight cast.
No stinging around my eyes.
Works better for me with moisturizer underneath.
evidence_notes: Sunscreen is foundational for pigmentation, retinoid use, and general photoprotection.
